SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF OCT-04-2021 - JUDGMENT. (PREVIOUSLY TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION ON OCT-04-2021)

THE COURT HAS ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF CRUZ MARTINEZ, MISAEL RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT FEDEX CORPORATION $6,000.00 PRINCIPAL, $75.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $6,075.00, *SEE ATTACHERD MC-025* STAY OF EXECUTION 30 DAYS PLAINTIFF'S AND DEFENDANT'S EXHIBITS RECEIVED

SMALL CLAIMS APPEAL CALENDAR OF JAN-20-2022 - SMALL CLAIMS JUDGMENT REVERSED. FEDERAL EXPRESS CORPORATION DOES NOT OWE MISAEL MARTINEZ ANY MONEY. THE JUDGMENT ON APPEAL IS FINAL AND REPLACES THE SMALL CLAIMS JUDGMENT FILED ON OCT-06-2021. JUDGE: CURTIS E. A.KARNOW, CLERK: K. SEPTIEN, NOT REPORTED, (611).
RECORD REMANDED TO SMALL CLAIMS (DEFENDANT EXHIBIT RETURNED TO SMALL CLAIMS DIVISION)

SMALL CLAIMS CALENDAR OF OCT-04-2021 - TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION AS OF OCT-04-2021 BY MICHELLE TONG. PLAINTIFF MISAEL CRUZ MARTINEZ IS PRESENT. DEFENDANT FEDEX CORPORATION REPRESENTED BY TIM MONIZ, IS PRESENT. DEFENDANT WAIVES SERVICE. COURT DISCLOSURE REGARDING CAMPAIGN CONTRIBUTIONS IS MADE. PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT EXHIBITS RETAINED. EMAIL FORM MAR CRUZ SENT TO DEPARTMENT506@SFTC.ORG IS RECIEVED AND PRINTED FOR REVIEW BY THE COURT. TRUPEK. NOT REPORTED. (506)

calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Jan-21-2022 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Misael Cruz Martinez Plaintiff/Respondent in pro per.
Attorney, Christopher Ahearn is present and representing Defendant/Appellant FedEx
Tim Moniz witness for Defendant/Appellant

All parties and witnesses are sworn.

Mr. Martinez Plaintiff/Respondent testifies on his own behalf.
Mr. Ahearn representing Defendant/Appellant cross-examines Plaintiff.

Mr. Ahearn representing Defendant/Appellant requests parties enter stipulation to rename defendant FedEx Corporation to Federal Express Corporation, stating that should the Judgement be entered in Mr. Martinezs favor the renamed Defendant, Federal Express Corporation, shall honor the Judgement.

Plaintiff agrees to the Stipulation.

Mr. Ahearn representing Defendant/Appellant conducts a direct examination of Tim Moniz.

Mr. Ahearn presents an exhibit packet containing tracking, Courrier information, recipient signature information, and package tracing information, and package inquiry information.

Plaintiff states his rebuttal.

Defendants Exhibits are kept in the file.

At the conclusion of the hearing the Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Curtis E. A. Karnow, Clerk: K. Septien, Not reported, (611).
